paragraphs: I work at the intersection of legal advisory, corporate governance, and — increasingly — applied AI.

Since 2021 I've held company secretary and administrative roles across a portfolio of real estate, construction, and investment entities in Accra, drafting governing documents, coordinating statutory filings, and advising boards on compliance with Ghana's corporate and land law regimes. In parallel, I serve as Chief Legal Counsel to the Kings University College Student Representative Council, representing student government before disciplinary and judicial bodies.

I'm not yet a qualified lawyer. I've gained admission to the Pre-Bar Course at the Ghana School of Law, the transitional step into Law Practice Training under Ghana's new legal education regime — and this month joined MIB Consults as a part-time Junior Legal Advisor. Alongside that, I'm reading for an LLM in International Law, Security & Diplomacy at the University of Gold Coast and have completed Google's AI Professional and AI Essentials certificates, which I'm now applying to legal-tech consulting and AI-assisted research.
